instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertion on How Pension Plans in India Work? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: pension plans in india, best pension plan in india, retirement calculator in india
Body Content:
Since their inception, pension plans have undergone a sea change in India. Previously, plans such as these were purchased as insurance products. You could withdraw the sum accumulated in a pension account as and when you needed it, provided you paid all your premiums up to the withdrawal date. These days, pension plans in India represent a method to save for your retirement, a way to get a secure income in your golden years. More or less, in India, pension plans offered by finance corporations and insurance companies work in the same way. Knowing how the structure and function of any plan will give you a general idea of how you can benefit from this way of saving.
Pension Plans in India – In Brief
You may think you are opting for the best pension plan in India, but most plans, across the board, offer you structures which are similar. Any pension plan essentially consists of two parts. One is accumulation of funds, and the other is distribution of funds. The way a pension plan works is that you have to make premium payments once a year, depending on the term chosen. This is further invested in an asset of your choosing, after the deduction of certain charges.
During the vesting time, that is, the time of granting the right to receive any pension benefits, you can request your insurer to begin your annuity/pension or withdraw your funds and buy a pension plan from the very same insurer. In case you do not wish to begin the pension instantly, you are able to buy one premium as a deferred pension product, or you may delay the age of vesting. You also have the choice to commute a one-third share of the fund on the vesting date. You receive this free of any tax liability. The amount that remains has to be used to buy annuity.
Compulsory Features in Pension Plans in India
There are certain features of pension plans in India, and these let you understand how these plans work to your benefit:
A Guaranteed Benefit on Maturity - Any pension plan you choose to avail of should ideally have a guaranteed benefit at maturity. This would amount to the value of your fund, or 101% of the total premium which has been paid. The higher of the two will be your payout benefit. This feature gives you the assurance that your funds remain safe.
A Guaranteed Benefit Upon Death - You can use a retirement calculator in India to determine how much of a benefit you should receive from your pension plan. You may then decide on premiums to be paid. Besides a benefit payout at retirement, your pension plan has the provision to give you an assured death benefit. This amounts to 105% of all the premiums paid, and additional amounts if you have made any top-up payments. In case of the untimely death of the holder of the plan, a nominee will duly receive this payout. If the policy has a “discontinued status”, the nominee duly receives the funds which remain before discontinuing the policy. Upon receipt of the death benefit, the nominee may choose one of three ways to use funds. The total death benefit may be withdrawn, the death benefit may be used to purchase another pension plan, or part of it may be used to buy a pension plan and part of it may be withdrawn.
Surrender of the Plan - In previous times, premiums had to be forfeited due to non-payment by policyholders. However, now, pension plans in India do not have this feature. Policyholders can discontinue premium payments, or even opt to surrender a pension plan, whenever they wish to. If you do this within the initial five years of signing up, then you will earn 4% per year on the funds remaining. You can withdraw funds after a five-year period. However, based on new rules, the proceeds have to be used to buy an annuity plan.
Conclusion
There is no best pension plan in India, as most of these are offered with similar structures and features. You cannot ignore any planning for your retirement, as this will be the time in your life that you need funds the most. Moreover, when you make your plans, you have to consider inflation too. Besides pension plans, there are other kinds of plans you can look at to secure your future, and these include life insurance plans to protect your loved ones and health insurance to protect you from the rising costs of health.

(Context: SEO Insertion on How Pension Plans in India Work? - Article Page)
